Forest – beesayatv https://beesayatv.com/ Documenting Cebu trails, one quiet hike at a time. Thu, 23 Jul 2026 04:43:07 +0000 en-US hourly 1 https://wordpress.org/?v=7.0.2 https://beesayatv.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/cropped-09-32x32.png Forest – beesayatv https://beesayatv.com/ 32 32 Camp 7 Farmland Trails | Minglanilla Part 1| 2026 GPX https://beesayatv.com/trails/camp-7-lubas-farm-trails/ Fri, 26 Jun 2026 04:12:27 +0000 https://beesayatv.com/?post_type=trail&p=277 Camp 7 Farm Trails is a point-to-point hike through the farmland of Camp 7, Lubas. The route follows rolling countryside, gradual elevation changes, and two small river crossings before finishing at the endpoint. The terrain is generally gentle and non-technical, making it suitable for beginners, although much of the hike is exposed to the sun.

Trail Snapshot

EASY
View others →
DocumentedMay 2026
Distance6.07 km (Point-to-Point)
Elevation Gain90 m
Duration1.5 - 2 hrs
WaterAt least 2 liters
ShadeLimited
Surface100% trail
GPXSupporter Access

Access

The documented hike began from a roadside location identified in the GPX track. Motorcycle parking was available along the road, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. The jump-off was reached via a concrete road. Private vehicles could access the area, while public transportation was limited. Habal-habal services were available.

Why this trail is rated Easy

✓ Easy-to-follow trail
✓ No technical climbing required
✓ Short hiking distance
✓ Mostly downhill route
✓ River crossings are simple

On this trail: farmland • forest • river crossing • viewpoint

Route Description

The route passes through elevated farmland with mostly gentle to moderate uphill sections before transitioning into a longer downhill stretch toward the end of the hike. The terrain rolls gradually through open countryside and includes two small river crossings along the way.

Although the elevation changes are moderate, the extended downhill section may feel tiring near the end of the route. Most of the trail is exposed, with very little shade available.

Trail Conditions

Conditions during the documented hike were sunny and dry. The trail was dry at the time but can become muddy during rainy weather. Shade coverage was very limited, and no stores were encountered along the route.

Mobile signal was strong near the start before gradually weakening farther along the trail. At least 2 liters of water was recommended. Very few people and very few insects were encountered during the hike.

Additional Notes

The hike begins on a road that was under construction during the documented hike. This section can become very muddy after rain.

The route crosses two small rivers. One crossing may become significantly deeper after heavy rainfall. At the time of recording (May 2026), a cement bridge was under construction at this location.

]]> Bitlang Trail Loop | Sudlon 1, Cebu City | 2026 GPX https://beesayatv.com/trails/bitlang-trail-loop/ Thu, 25 Jun 2026 10:03:27 +0000 https://beesayatv.com/?post_type=trail&p=205 Bitlang Trail Loop is a documented hiking route in the uplands of Sudlon 1, Cebu City. The route descends via Bitlang Trail toward Bonbon before following the road to Buot-Taup. From there, the hike returns to Sitio Tabla through a sustained uphill climb, completing a loop of approximately 12.9 km. While the descent is generally straightforward, the prolonged ascent from Buot-Taup is the defining challenge of the route and accounts for its Tough rating.

Trail Snapshot

TOUGH
View others →
DocumentedJune 2026
Distance12.89 km (Loop)
Elevation Gain602 m
Duration6-7 hrs
WaterAt least 3 liters
ShadeModerate
Surface60% trail, 40% road
GPXSupporter Access

Access

The documented hike began near the covered court in Sitio Tabla, which was reached via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. Private vehicles could access the jump-off point. V-hires were available along the Transcentral Highway route, and habal-habal services were also available.

Why this trail is rated Tough

✓ Long hiking distance requiring sustained endurance
✓ Long or sustained climbs and descents
✓ Prolonged physical effort over several hours
✓ High route commitment with limited bailout options
✓ Assistance or evacuation may take considerable time

On this trail: farmland • forest • ridge • valley • viewpoint

Route Description

The route begins in Sitio Tabla before descending through narrow trails, farmland, small settlements, and river sections toward Bonbon. After leaving the trail, the route follows the local road to Buot-Taup, where the character of the hike changes. From Buot-Taup, a sustained uphill climb returns to Sitio Tabla, completing the documented loop. Although the descent is generally straightforward, the prolonged ascent requires significantly greater endurance and is the defining challenge of the hike.

Trail Conditions

Conditions during the documented hike were cloudy throughout the day. Rain the night before left portions of the trail slightly muddy. The route alternated between shaded forest sections and more exposed farmland and road segments. Stores were available near local communities before and during the hike.

Mobile signal was strong near the start but became unavailable in parts of the route. Hikers completing the full loop should carry at least 3 liters of water, particularly during warmer weather when the prolonged ascent from Buot-Taup can significantly increase physical exertion. No other hikers were encountered during the documented hike, while mosquitoes were present in sections with dense vegetation.

Additional Notes

This report documents the complete Bitlang Trail Loop. The accompanying video, however, covers only the approximately 5 km descending section of Bitlang Trail. The return from Buot-Taup to Sitio Tabla involves a sustained uphill climb of approximately 4 km and is not shown in the video.

Stores are available between the Bitlang Trail exit and the beginning of the return ascent. On sunny days, an umbrella may be helpful, as much of the route is exposed with limited tree cover.

The route has no dangerous cliffs or exposed ridges. A walking stick or trekking pole may help improve balance on uneven terrain and reduce strain on the knees, particularly during the prolonged return climb.

]]> Alpaco Trail Loop | Alpaco, Naga City | 2026 GPX https://beesayatv.com/trails/alpaco-trail-loop/ Wed, 24 Jun 2026 14:13:01 +0000 https://beesayatv.com/?post_type=trail&p=125 Alpaco Trail Loop is a 12 km loop hike in Pinamungahan, Cebu, connecting Veloso Lake, Purok Hoybesan, Bandilaan, and Alpaco. The route follows a combination of rural roads and community trails with straightforward navigation. While the terrain is generally non-technical, the distance and extended exposed sections require moderate physical effort.

Trail Snapshot

MILD
View others →
DocumentedJune 2026
Distance12 km (Loop)
Elevation Gain244 m
Duration3-4 hrs
WaterAt least 2 liters
ShadeModerate
Surface20% trail, 80% road
GPXSupporter Access

Access

The documented hike began near the trail leading to Veloso Lake, which was reached via a concrete road. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. Private vehicles could access the jump-off point, while public transportation was very limited. Habal-habal services were available.

The documented jump-off provided the quickest access to Veloso Lake but required a short hike, as four-wheel vehicles could not pass the narrow access trail. The other documented approaches to the lake were via Sibago and Alpaco.

Why this trail is rated Mild

✓ Mostly easy-to-follow trail
✓ Occasional people encountered
✓ Longer hiking distance
✓ Long exposed sections with little shade
✓ Limited turnaround points

On this trail: forest • lake • roads • viewpoint

Route Description

The route follows a long but straightforward loop through Veloso Lake, Purok Hoybesan, Bandilaan, and Alpaco. Navigation was easy throughout the hike, with occasional encounters with local residents. Most of the route was non-technical, although the overall distance and long exposed sections made the hike more demanding, particularly during hot weather.

Approximately 80% of the route followed concrete roads with very little shade. Habal-habal vehicles regularly passed through these roads, making it possible to shorten the hike if necessary.

Veloso Lake forms part of the route, although the documented hike treats it as a short detour within the larger loop.

Trail Conditions

Conditions during the documented hike were cloudy, with a mix of dry and muddy trail sections. Most of the route was exposed, with limited shade. Stores were available near several communities along the route.

Mobile signal was strong near the start before becoming intermittent farther along the route. Between 1 and 2 liters of water was recommended. Few people and minimal insect activity were observed during the hike.

Additional Notes

Parts of the route passed through pig-raising areas where strong livestock odors were observed.

Several dogs were encountered, particularly near homes and communities. Carrying a hiking stick and giving dogs adequate space was recommended during the documented hike.

The route passes through several communities where the local residents encountered during the hike were observed to be friendly and approachable.

]]>
